perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that, for a period of one year after the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by Provider, neither it nor any of its affiliates who have been provided Evaluation Material, will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or all or substantially all of the assets of Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Provider or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of Provider;
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that, for a period of six months after the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by Provider, neither it nor any of its affiliates who have been provided Evaluation Material, will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or all or substantially all of the assets of Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Provider or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of Provider;
perturbation.explanation: Changing the standstill period from one year to six months creates an in-text contradiction within Section 9 itself, because the definition of the Standstill Period is dependent on what is stated in the first line of the section. This introduces ambiguity regarding the duration of the standstill agreement. Is it one year, or six months, according to the new changes?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that, for a period of six months after the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by Provider, neither it nor any of its affiliates who have been provided Evaluation Material, will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or all or substantially all of the assets of Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Provider or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of Provider; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the 1934 Act) with respect to the securities of Provider; (c) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit an unsolicited proposal for or offer of (with or without condition), any extraordinary transaction involving Provider or its equity securities or assets; (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of Provider; (e) take any action which might force Provider to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (f) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing. Recipient also agrees during the Standstill Period not to publicly request (or request in a manner or under circumstances that would reasonably require public disclosure of such request) Provider (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, to amend or waive any provision of this Section 9 (including this sentence). Recipient further agrees that unless otherwise directed by Provider in writing (i) all communications with the Provider regarding a Possible Transaction, (ii) requests for additional information, facility tours, or management meetings, and (iii) discussions or questions regarding procedures with respect to a Possible Transaction, will be submitted or directed by Recipient or its Representatives only to Ethan Zweig of Citigroup Global Markets Inc., as Provider’s financial advisor, or a person or persons designated in writing by Mr. Zweig, unless otherwise agreed to by the Provider.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the Standstill Period from one year to six months. The contradicted text, which appears to be the changed text, also states "for a period of six months after the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”)". While they are the same, it indicates that the original agreement had a one-year standstill period, while now it's six months. Therefore, the definition of "Standstill Period" is inconsistent with the original text, creating an in-text contradiction as per the explanation provided, where the definition of Standstill Period has been changed from one year to six months. This falls under Inconsistencies as per the definition. This creates ambiguity regarding the duration of the standstill agreement, directly conflicting with the initial agreement of one year. This fits the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and Recipient hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until the Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a term sheet has been signed by both parties, and Recipient hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until the Parties shall have entered into a term sheet.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 12 to state that a 'term sheet' (instead of a 'final definitive agreement') establishes the existence of a Possible Transaction, directly contradicts the original intention of requiring a 'final definitive agreement.' This change causes confusion about the point at which a legally binding agreement is formed and how Recipient can waive claims relating to the existence of a possible transaction, especially considering a term sheet is less enforceable than a 'final definitive agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a term sheet has been signed by both parties, and Recipient hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until the Parties shall have entered into a term sheet. Recipient also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither of the Parties will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. None of the Parties are under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction and the Parties may terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 12 to state that a 'term sheet' establishes the existence of a Possible Transaction, while the original text required a 'final definitive agreement.' The contradicted text includes both the 'term sheet' and 'final definitive agreement' conditions, but it is clear that by weakening the original text to only require a 'term sheet' to create a legally binding agreement, the document now contains conflicting statements about when a legally binding agreement is formed. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections (the modified section and the contradicted section) now provide conflicting conditions for the existence of a contract, which creates uncertainty and is a strong in-text contradiction.
